thesis: Abacus Global Management is a life-settlement and alternative-asset platform on the NYSE. **[Fact]** It originates and services life insurance policies, runs asset-management sleeves (FCF Advisors, acquired December 2024), and sells mortality-verification technology through Abacus Intel. **[Fact]** FY2025 revenue reached $235.2M (+110% YoY) with adjusted EBITDA of $132.6M, while GAAP cash from operations was $25.7M because policy purchases absorb cash before mortality payoffs.
why_market_wrong: Life-settlement economics are opaque: GAAP free cash flow screens thin ($0.25/sh FY2025) while adjusted EBITDA and policy-portfolio marks tell a stronger operating story. **[Inference]** Complexity (fair-value portfolio, securitized funding, dual equity/note structure) may keep generalist investors away even when servicing fees and securitization are repeatable.

key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: FY2025 OCF $25.68M less capex $0.93M on 99.23M diluted shares (10-K filed 2026-03-13)
Five additive components with calculation_proof graphs (evidence_reconciliation_2026-07-21.md)
Life Solutions origination scale + asset-management fee growth; securitization lowers funding cost

open_questions: Whether adjusted EBITDA should inform owner-cash normalization for stance gate.
Post-acquisition ROIC on Carlisle and FCF Advisors integrations.
Non-recourse securitization carve-out vs full recourse debt in net financial claims.
Dividend sustainability at $0.20/sh vs lumpy GAAP cash flow.
